pages.text:
Talaphone:WHITEHALL 44日岁.

Telegraphic Address;- "OPPOSITELY, LESQUARE, LONDON."

P.S. 38/9/1

UNION OF SOUTH AFRICA.

SOUTH AFRICA HOUSE,

TRAFALGAR SQUARE,

LONDON, W. C. 2.

11th August, 1942.

Lane,

My dear M. Dejean:

With reference to my letter of the 9th

May, informing you of the Union Government's decision to recognise Colonel Pechkoff as the official delegate in the Union of South Africa of the French National

Committee, I have the honour, on instructions from ly

Government to inform you further that by the recognition so accorded to Colonel Pechkoff it should be understood that the Government of the Union of South Africa recognise the French National Committee under the presidency of

General de Gaulle as the de facto authority in the French

Territories and Territories under French Mandate which at present fall under the control of that Committee and recognise the said Delegate as the representative in the Union of South

Africa of that Committee and as representing those French citizens in the Union of south Africa, who identify themselves with the Fighting French Movement.

Monsieur m. DeJean

National Conmissioner of Foreisr

French National Committee,

4 Carlton Gardens

London. S.W.

Yours sin canny

COMITE NATIONAL FRANCAIS

Affairs Afain Franzires

Cabinet da Cominissaire National

• Enregisté 1e.13:8:.43

Dossier I. A 2. 7. 2
